title: eriogonum howellii var. argense
intro: Eriogonum howellii var. argense, commonly known as Howell's buckwheat or Howell's buckwheat sedge, is a plant species in the buckwheat family native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum howellii var. argense include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as skin irritations and infections. Eriogonum howellii var. argense can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made for medicinal use.
